title: payment — Eloquent observers
description: Four observers enforce the module's write-time invariants: (1) PaymentObserver enforces the parent-invoice + succeeded-intent contract + immutability + the three atomic side effects (Transaction.record + Invoice.mark_paid + marketing capture). (2) PaymentMethodObserver enforces the entitlement gate + fingerprint deduplication + PCI-DSS scope (no PAN). (3) PaymentIntentObserver enforces the state machine (one-way transitions) + 3DS2 gate + encryption. (4) PaymentDisputeObserver enforces the dispute lifecycle + evidence collection + escalation.

handles: creating → populate ULID prefix `pay_`
creating → refuse without parent invoice (returns `PAYMENT_MISSING_INVOICE`)
creating → refuse without parent payment_intent AND intent.status != 'succeeded' (returns `PAYMENT_INTENT_NOT_SUCCEEDED`)
creating → refuse when payment_intent already has a Payment row (unique on payment_intent_id — defense-in-depth against the unique index)
creating → generate payment_number sequentially per tenant via PostgreSQL advisory lock: 'PAY-YYYY-NNNNNNNN' (PaymentNumberGenerator)
creating → freeze billing_details_snapshot from the intent's customer profile at capture time (jsonb)
creating → compute net_cents = amount_cents - fee_cents (denormalised for reporting)
creating → refuse when payment_method_id set AND method.tenant_id != payment.tenant_id (cross-tenant FK smuggling)
creating → refuse when payment_method_id set AND method.is_active = false (returns `PAYMENT_METHOD_INACTIVE`)
creating → validate device_fingerprint_hash matches SHA-256 pattern when set
creating → refuse when payment_method_last4 is set to anything other than exactly 4 digits (PCI-DSS Level 1 — never full PAN)
creating → encrypt provider_metadata at rest

created (after commit) → dispatch three atomic side effects IN THE SAME DB TRANSACTION as the row insert (via observer.creating running inside the wrapping transaction): (1) TransactionRecorder.record($payment) — double-entry: debit cash, credit accounts_receivable; sets transaction_id on the row. (2) Invoice.mark_paid($payment) — parent invoice.status = 'paid' (full) or 'partially_paid' (partial). (3) Fire domain event `finance.membership_purchased` when invoice.membership_id is set — growth::marketing's MarketingEventCapturer picks it up.
created (after commit) → fire PaymentCaptured event
created (after commit) → update parent PaymentMethod.last_used_at when payment_method_id set (async — separate job, not transactional-critical)

updating → REFUSE ALL FIELDS except amount_refunded_cents + amount_disputed_cents + updated_at + transaction_id (transaction_id may be set once transaction recording completes; otherwise every field frozen). Any other mutation returns `PAYMENT_IMMUTABLE`.
updating (amount_refunded_cents changed) → refuse when new value > amount_cents (returns `PAYMENT_REFUND_EXCEEDS_CAPTURE`)
updating (amount_refunded_cents changed) → validate the change is triggered by the refund module (via cross-module event bus) — direct mutation is refused
deleting → REFUSE ALL — payments are immutable financial records. Hard-purged by PurgeExpiredPaymentDataJob only after retention window closes (7y baseline; 10y Enterprise).
creating → populate ULID prefix `pmt_`
creating → refuse when tenant does NOT hold `payment_capture` entitlement (returns `PAYMENT_FEATURE_NOT_ENTITLED`)
creating → refuse when tenant does NOT hold `payment_saved_methods` entitlement (returns `PAYMENT_SAVED_METHODS_NOT_ENTITLED`)
creating → validate provider is one of the 6 supported enums (returns `PAYMENT_INVALID_PROVIDER`)

creating → validate provider_reference_id matches per-provider format (Stripe pm_..., Paddle payment_method_id, Adyen storedPaymentMethodId, PayPal payment_method, Square CARD_NONCE, custom_webhook caller-defined). Returns `PAYMENT_INVALID_PROVIDER_REFERENCE`.
creating → refuse when customer.tenant_id != payment_method.tenant_id (cross-tenant FK smuggling)
creating → validate customer_type IN ('user::User', 'sports::Athlete')
creating → validate last4 is exactly 4 digits when set (returns `PAYMENT_INVALID_LAST4` — never full PAN)
creating → validate exp_month IN [1, 12] + exp_year IN [current_year, current_year + 20] when set
creating → hash provider fingerprint into fingerprint_hash (SHA-256) — never store raw

creating → check for duplicate (same tenant + customer + fingerprint_hash) with deleted_at IS NULL: if found + is_active=false: re-activate the existing row + fire PaymentMethodDuplicateDetected + REFUSE the new insert. If found + is_active=true: refuse the new insert with `PAYMENT_METHOD_DUPLICATE` (returns the existing method_id).
creating → encrypt provider_reference_id at rest via TokenEncryptor (AES-256-GCM)
creating → when is_default=true: atomic swap — flip existing is_default=true rows to false for the same (tenant, customer) via lock-and-update
creating → fire PaymentMethodAttached
updating (is_default flipped true) → atomic swap (same as create path)
updating (is_active flipped false) → require deactivated_reason IS NOT NULL (returns `PAYMENT_METHOD_MISSING_DEACTIVATED_REASON`)

updating (is_active flipped false) → refuse when the method is currently referenced by an in-flight PaymentIntent (status != terminal) — the intent would break mid-flow. Returns `PAYMENT_METHOD_IN_USE`.
updating (verified_at set from NULL → now()) → allowed only when method_type = 'bank_debit' (returns `PAYMENT_METHOD_VERIFICATION_NOT_APPLICABLE` otherwise)
updating (provider / provider_reference_id / method_type / fingerprint_hash / customer_type / customer_id changed) → REFUSE — these are immutable after create

deleting (soft-delete) → dispatch provider-side detach (Stripe DetachPaymentMethod, Adyen storedPaymentMethod delete). NON-BLOCKING — provider detach failure warns + logs; the soft-delete succeeds regardless (state converges via reconciliation later)
deleting (soft-delete) → set is_active=false + deactivated_reason='customer_removed' when not already set
deleting (soft-delete) → fire PaymentMethodDetached
deleting (hard-delete) → allow only when soft-deleted > 90 days ago (grace period)
creating → populate ULID prefix `pit_`
creating → refuse when tenant does NOT hold `payment_capture` entitlement
creating → refuse without invoice + amount + currency
creating → refuse when amount_cents <= 0 (returns `PAYMENT_INVALID_POSITIVE_AMOUNT`)
creating → refuse when currency is not ISO 4217 (returns `PAYMENT_INVALID_CURRENCY`)
creating → refuse when currency != tenant.default_currency AND tenant does NOT hold `payment_multi_currency` entitlement (returns `PAYMENT_MULTI_CURRENCY_NOT_ENTITLED`)
creating → refuse when capture_method = 'manual' AND tenant does NOT hold `payment_manual_capture` entitlement (returns `PAYMENT_MANUAL_CAPTURE_NOT_ENTITLED`)
creating → refuse when invoice.status IN ('paid', 'void', 'uncollectible', 'refunded') — cannot create intent against terminal invoice (returns `PAYMENT_INVOICE_TERMINAL`)

creating → refuse when an in-flight PaymentIntent already exists for the same invoice (status IN non-terminal) — the module enforces one active intent per invoice (returns `PAYMENT_INTENT_ALREADY_IN_FLIGHT`)
creating → dispatch provider.createIntent() to get provider_reference_id + client_secret + next_action_data
creating → encrypt provider_reference_id + client_secret at rest via TokenEncryptor (distinct keys for defense-in-depth)
creating → set status='initialized'
creating → set default confirmation_method='automatic' unless overridden
creating → fire PaymentIntentCreated

updating (status transitions) → enforce state machine: initialized → {requires_action, requires_payment_method, requires_confirmation, processing}; requires_action → {requires_confirmation, requires_capture, processing, cancelled, failed}; requires_payment_method → requires_confirmation; requires_confirmation → {requires_action, requires_capture, processing, cancelled, failed}; requires_capture → {succeeded, cancelled}; processing → {succeeded, failed, cancelled}; succeeded/cancelled/failed are TERMINAL. Any invalid transition returns `PAYMENT_INTENT_INVALID_TRANSITION`.
updating (status → requires_action) → require next_action_type IS NOT NULL (returns `PAYMENT_INTENT_NEXT_ACTION_MISSING`); fire PaymentIntentRequiresAction
updating (status → processing) → fire PaymentIntentProcessing
updating (status → succeeded) → set succeeded_at = now(); atomically create Payment row via PaymentProcessor::createPayment($intent) in the same DB transaction; fire PaymentIntentSucceeded
updating (status → failed) → require last_error_code + last_error_type set; fire PaymentIntentFailed; keep parent invoice.status unchanged so retry can happen
updating (status → cancelled) → set cancelled_at + cancellation_reason; fire PaymentIntentCancelled
updating (provider / provider_reference_id / amount_cents / currency / capture_method / confirmation_method changed) → REFUSE — immutable after create
updating (payment_method_id set from NULL) → validate method.is_active = true + method.tenant_id = intent.tenant_id + method.customer resolves to the intent's customer
deleting → REFUSE unless status IS terminal (succeeded / cancelled / failed) AND retention window elapsed (90 days). Terminal intents are hard-purged by PurgeExpiredPaymentDataJob.
creating → populate ULID prefix `pdi_`
creating → refuse when tenant does NOT hold `payment_dispute_management` entitlement (returns `PAYMENT_DISPUTE_MANAGEMENT_NOT_ENTITLED`)
creating → refuse without parent payment (returns `PAYMENT_DISPUTE_MISSING_PAYMENT`)
creating → refuse when parent payment.tenant_id != dispute.tenant_id (cross-tenant FK smuggling)
creating → refuse when parent payment.provider != dispute.provider (dispute must originate from the same provider that captured)
creating → validate reason IS one of the 9 enums
creating → validate amount_cents <= payment.amount_cents when currency matches (partial disputes allowed)
creating → validate currency = payment.currency (defense-in-depth)
creating → encrypt provider_reference_id + evidence at rest
creating → set status = 'needs_response' unless explicitly warning_* (provider webhook determines)
creating → set opened_at = now()
creating → atomically update payment.amount_disputed_cents += amount_cents (parent counter)
creating → dispatch CollectDisputeEvidenceJob (auto-collects invoice + membership + notification history for evidence pack)
creating → dispatch NotifyDisputeEvidenceDueJob delayed by (evidence_due_by - 3 days) when evidence_due_by is set
creating → fire PaymentDisputeOpened (P1 for high-value: > $500 configurable via payment.dispute.high_value_threshold_cents)

updating (status transitions) → enforce state machine: warning_needs_response → {warning_under_review, warning_closed, needs_response}; warning_under_review → {warning_closed, needs_response}; warning_closed is TERMINAL; needs_response → {under_review, charge_refunded}; under_review → {won, lost}; charge_refunded / won / lost are TERMINAL. Any invalid transition returns `PAYMENT_DISPUTE_INVALID_TRANSITION`.
updating (evidence_submitted_at set from NULL → now()) → validate evidence jsonb has at least one non-null field
updating (evidence_submitted_at set) → fire PaymentDisputeEvidenceSubmitted
updating (status → won) → set closed_at + outcome='won' + revert payment.amount_disputed_cents; fire PaymentDisputeClosed + PaymentDisputeWon

updating (status → lost) → set closed_at + outcome='lost'; fire PaymentDisputeClosed + PaymentDisputeLost; dispatch EscalateDisputeToChargebackJob (creates Chargeback row via chargeback module + populates dispute.chargeback_id); fire PaymentDisputeEscalatedToChargeback after chargeback creation

updating (status → charge_refunded) → set closed_at + outcome='accepted'; require refund_id populated (refund module dispatches provider-side refund via POST /disputes/{dispute}/accept); fire PaymentDisputeClosed
updating (opened_at / provider / provider_reference_id / reason / amount_cents / currency changed) → REFUSE — immutable after create
updating (chargeback_id set from NULL) → allowed only when outcome='lost' AND status='lost'
updating (refund_id set from NULL) → allowed only when outcome='accepted' AND status='charge_refunded'
updating (closed_at reset to NULL) → REFUSE — dispute closure is one-way
deleting → REFUSE ALL — dispute records are financial audit; retained per financial retention window (7y baseline; 10y Enterprise)
